question: Limbikani says he is not scared ________ this year’s examinations.
explanation: We always use "of" after the word "scared" to show what makes someone afraid.

question: Mapulanga was entrusted ________ the responsibility of keeping the organization’s funds.
explanation: We use "with" after "entrusted" when we give someone a job or something to take care of.

question: The choir was praised because of singing songs fit ________ the occasion.
explanation: We use "for" with "fit" to say that something is right or good for a special event.

question: The club decided to relieve the coach ________ his duties following the team’s poor performance.
explanation: To "relieve someone of" duties means to take away their job or responsibility.

question: “Do not be anxious ________ anything,” the new head teacher advised us.
explanation: We use "about" with "anxious" to talk about the thing that makes us worry.

question: The two gentlemen parted ways because they failed to agree ________ the price of the commodity.
explanation: We use "on" with "agree" when people are talking about a specific price or plan.

question: Temperance and employment are conducive ________ health.
explanation: "Conducive to" means that something helps make a good result happen, like good health.

question: The goat subsists ________ the coarsest of food.
explanation: To "subsist on" means to use a certain food to stay alive.

question: The teacher caught the student while he was peeping ________ the window.
explanation: We use "through" to describe looking into a place from an opening like a window.

question: The athlete persisted ________ finishing the race despite a serious ankle injury.
explanation: To "persist in" means to keep doing something even when it is very hard.

question: “I didn’t find any problem since I am acquainted ________ the latest development,” said Mtendere.
explanation: To be "acquainted with" means that you know about something or someone.

question: The twin’s resemblance ________ each other amazed the entire village.
explanation: We use "to" after "resemblance" to show that two people look like each other.

question: Most students are now adhering ________ the school’s revised rules.
explanation: To "adhere to" means to follow rules or laws very carefully.

question: It is safer to hold a live snake ________ its tail.
explanation: We use "by" to name the specific part of an object that someone is holding.

question: Mwayi and Tamara parted ways because they disagreed ________ the distribution of the profits.
explanation: We say "disagreed over" when people have a fight about a specific topic or plan.

question: Chilu’s laziness is likely to result ________ poor performance if he does not change.
explanation: To "result in" means that one action will cause a certain ending to happen.

question: The candidate was just halfway ________ the paper when he collapsed in the examination room.
explanation: We use "through" to say someone is in the middle of finishing a task or document.

question: He has been advised to give up driving because ________ his failing eyesight.
explanation: "Because of" is a set of words used to give the reason for something.

question: There is really no difference ________ you and me regarding this matter.
explanation: We use "between" when we are talking about exactly two people or things.

question: The organizers warned all the athletes ________ breaching the rules of the new competition.
explanation: To "warn against" something means to tell someone not to do a bad or dangerous act.

question: The bus was bound ________ Blantyre when it had a mechanical breakdown.
explanation: We use "for" with "bound" to show the place where a person or vehicle is going.

question: Is there a permanent cure ________ the disease that has hit the livestock?
explanation: We use "for" with the word "cure" to name the medicine or help for a sickness.

question: The player was disqualified ________ competing after failing a drug test.
explanation: To be "disqualified from" means you are not allowed to take part in a race or game.

question: He is incapable ________ doing good work without constant supervision.
explanation: We always use "of" after "incapable" to show that someone cannot do a certain task.

question: The teacher impressed ________ us the value of hard work.
explanation: To "impress on" people means to make them understand that an idea is very important.

question: I cannot mistake her ________ her sister because they look very different.
explanation: To "mistake for" means to think one person is actually someone else.

question: The old man is greatly afflicted ________ the loss of his only child.
explanation: To be "afflicted with" means to suffer from a problem, a sickness, or a great sadness.

question: His statement to the police was tantamount ________ a confession of guilt.
explanation: "Tantamount to" is a formal way to say that one thing is the same as another thing.

question: The public is cautioned ________ pickpockets in crowded trading centers.
explanation: To be "cautioned against" means to be told to watch out for a danger.

question: The manager was found guilty ________ manslaughter by the High Court.
explanation: We use "of" after the word "guilty" to name the specific crime that was done.

question: All citizens are expected to abide ________ the laws of the country.
explanation: To "abide by" means to follow and obey the laws or the rules.

question: Many young people are currently addicted ________ social media.
explanation: We use "to" after "addicted" to show the thing that a person cannot stop using.

question: We were all amazed ________ the speed with which the technician fixed the computer.
explanation: We use "at" with "amazed" to show the thing that caused a very big surprise.

question: Farmers in our area will benefit ________ the new irrigation scheme.
explanation: To "benefit from" means to get help or something good from a plan or project.

question: The two brothers are very different ________ each other in character.
explanation: We say "different from" to show how two people or things are not the same.

question: Dalitso continues to excel ________ mathematics and science.
explanation: To "excel in" a subject means to be very good at it and get high marks.

question: She is very good ________ explaining complex grammar rules to her classmates.
explanation: We say someone is "good at" an activity when they have the skill to do it well.

question: A truly great leader is often indifferent ________ both praise and blame.
explanation: Being "indifferent to" something means you do not care about it or it does not change you.

question: “Please be kind ________ animals,” the nature guide advised the students.
explanation: We use "to" after the word "kind" to show who is receiving the good behavior.

question: The new head teacher is very popular ________ all the members of staff.
explanation: If you are "popular with" a group, it means that the people in that group like you.

question: In most government departments, a director is senior ________ a principal secretary.
explanation: We use "to" instead of "than" when comparing people with words like "senior" or "junior."

question: The player was sorry ________ being late for the final practice session.
explanation: We say "sorry for" when we want to show regret for something we did wrong.

question: If you persevere, you will eventually succeed ________ your business.
explanation: To "succeed in" means to do well and reach your goals in a job or business.

question: The imported wheat is superior ________ the locally produced variety.
explanation: "Superior to" means that one thing is better than another thing.

question: Everyone was surprised ________ the sudden resignation of the general manager.
explanation: We use "at" with "surprised" to show the reason why people are shocked.

question: The police still suspect him ________ involvement in the robbery.
explanation: We use "of" after "suspect" to link a person to a crime the police think they did.

question: We all sympathize ________ the family that lost their home in the fire.
explanation: To "sympathize with" means to feel sorry for someone else’s pain or loss.

question: “Don’t leave yet; I am still waiting ________ my exam results,” said Tiyamike.
explanation: We use "for" after "waiting" to show the person or thing we are expecting to arrive.

question: The examination committee consists ________ ten experienced teachers.
explanation: "Consists of" is used to list the parts or the people that make up a whole group.

question: Your success in the MSCE exams will depend ________ your hard work.
explanation: To "depend on" means that one thing is decided or changed by another thing.
